title: Slight increase in unemployment rate in March
content: Overall Labour Force Participation Rate among persons aged 15 and above was 55.4% in March, lower than 55.9% in February. The rural LFPR was 58% in March compared to 58.7% in previous month.

title: Amravati case: Teens ‘bet’ to win over girls turned into voyeuristic fetish of recording acts
content: A dispute between two accused, Ayan Ahmed and Uzer Khan, exposed a sexual abuse and blackmail scandal involving girls in Amravati. Their alleged bet to win over girls escalated to recording and uploading explicit videos online. The fallout reportedly occurred over money, leading Uzer Khan to make the videos viral in anger.

title: CSK knock BCCI's door, lodge complaint against RCB for 'Dosa idli' song
content: Chennai Super Kings have lodged a complaint with the BCCI against Royal Challengers Bengaluru over alleged misconduct during their IPL 2026 clash. The complaint cites inappropriate in-stadium entertainment, including a song that CSK felt stereotyped their identity, and remarks made by the stadium DJ after player dismissals. This adds to the growing rivalry between the two franchises.

title: Explained: How Vaishali caged Lagno's 'dragon' to claim historic Women's Candidates title
content: Playing white pieces while chasing a result, players often get greedy and make more adventurous, attacking moves on the board, weakening their defence in the process. Vaishali had white pieces. She was up against Russia's Kateryna Lagno, a mother of four and quite brilliant at keeping a poker face on the table. Vaishali had to be brave to overcome the challenge posed by the veteran, and she did so with a clinical precision that belied the stakes.

title: ‘Gave up my job to care’: Bengaluru man throws 75-year-old mother to death from 4th floor
content: A Bengaluru man, Venkatesh C, has been arrested for allegedly throwing his paralysed mother from their fourth-floor rented home, killing her instantly. He confessed to police that caring for his bedridden mother, who suffered a stroke five years ago, had become unbearable. The tragic incident unfolded in RR Nagar on Wednesday afternoon.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ box office day 28: Film misses Rs 1,100 mark by a whisker
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' has achieved a historic Rs 1,099.72 crore net in India, becoming the first Hindi film to cross the Rs 1,000 crore mark in a single language. Despite minor fluctuations, the espionage thriller has maintained a strong box office presence for 28 days, with a worldwide gross of Rs 1,733.20 crore.

title: Comedy of errors in PSL! Chaos unfolds as run-out chance goes begging - Watch
content: The incident took place in the final over of Quetta’s innings. Ali Raza bowled a waist-high no-ball to Usman Tariq on the fourth delivery. On the next ball, which was a free hit, Abrar Ahmed was on strike.

title: Lawyers in America to clients: Stop talking to ChatGPT and Claude
content: US lawyers are warning clients against sharing sensitive legal information with AI chatbots like ChatGPT and Claude. A recent court ruling indicated such conversations might not be protected by attorney-client privilege and could be used as evidence in legal cases. This development raises significant concerns about client confidentiality and potential legal risks.

title: Anushka Sharma's reaction goes viral as Virat Kohli misses fifty vs LSG - Watch
content: Chasing 147, Kohli got RCB off to a strong start and scored 49 off 34 balls, hitting six fours and one six. He looked set for his 66th IPL half-century but was dismissed on 49, caught by Nicholas Pooran off Avesh Khan while attempting a shot in the air towards long-on.

title: CBSE's second board exam for Class 10 private candidates: How to apply and other details
content: CBSE has introduced a Two Board Examination Scheme for Class 10, set to be implemented in 2026, allowing students to improve scores in up to three subjects through a second exam in May. The board has outlined strict eligibility, online-only application rules, defined timelines, and a structured fee system, alongside stringent compliance and verification measures.

title: Oil price today: Crude dips to $90 amid hopes of Middle East de escalation
content: Oil prices eased as hopes for de-escalation in US-Iran tensions emerged, with Iran suggesting passage through the Omani side of the Strait of Hormuz if an agreement is reached. Despite optimism for diplomatic progress, the US warned of increased economic pressure if Iran remains defiant, while waivers for Iranian oil purchases will not be extended.

title: TCS Nashik case: Police says men acted like organised gang to target female colleagues
content: Nashik police have filed nine FIRs against IT firm employees for alleged religious conversion attempts, sexual harassment, and workplace misconduct. Investigators believe seven male employees acted as an organized group targeting female staff. An HR head was arrested for discouraging a victim. Investigations into potential links with extremist groups are ongoing.

title: ‘Rented room, 100 videos’: How Maharashtra teen 'lured, abused minors'; chilling details emerge
content: A 19-year-old arrested for sexually abusing and blackmailing minors faces property demolition as authorities razed a part of his family home. Police are investigating the circulation of over 100 videos and have identified eight survivors, urging them to come forward.

title: Travis Kelce’s ring for Taylor Swift sparks rare antique diamond jewelry trend
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have reignited interest in antique diamonds, shifting focus from flawless, mass-produced stones to rare, character-rich pieces. Their engagement spotlighted vintage craftsmanship, sustainability, and uniqueness. Jewellers report rising demand, though high-quality antique diamonds remain scarce and costly. As buyers seek meaning over perfection, these historic stones are becoming prized choices, blending romance, heritage, and individuality in modern engagement trends.

title: NHL Playoffs: Lightning vs Canadiens opener pushed due to arena scheduling
content: Playoff opener between the Montreal Canadiens and Tampa bay lightening has been postponed because of issues with arena availability and the broadcast schedule. The shift moves Game 1 to Sunday to give both teams more time to prepare. Having rested key players and having all logistics done, the next question is how the re-worked schedule would affect the early momentum in this much-hyped NHL game.

title: Connor McDavid $20 million contract projection: Impact on Maple Leafs roster strategy
content: The most recent NHL news line underscores the sense that Connor McDavid would earn $20 million in the projected contract and how this has increasingly become an issue in Toronto Maple Leafs planning. Toronto seems to be getting its long-term salary cap and roster plan on track, as the Edmonton Oilers superstar keeps controlling. The case is indicative of larger NHL trade theories and new practices in team-building based on generational talent acquisition.

title: White House denies US sought truce extension, says new talks may happen in Pakistan
content: The White House denied formally requesting a ceasefire extension with Iran, despite ongoing diplomatic engagements. While cautiously optimistic about a deal, the US has made its "red lines" clear. Meanwhile, Pakistan's army chief visited Tehran with a potential new framework for talks, following previous negotiations that stalled over key issues like Iran's nuclear program.

title: ‘I’m still not 100%’: Virat Kohli’s blunt fitness admission after RCB win
content: Virat Kohli's unbeaten 49 steered Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a five-wicket victory over Lucknow Super Giants. Despite the match-winning knock, Kohli revealed he's still not at peak fitness, battling a sore knee and illness. He emphasised the team's adaptable batting depth and the importance of smart play as the IPL season progresses.

title: ‘Vinod Kambli at risk of brain stroke’: Close friend reveals a clot in his brain
content: Vinod Kambli continues to face health issues due to a brain clot affecting his memory, with doctors warning smoking could risk a stroke. His friend Marcus Couto shared concerns, though Kambli shows signs of recovery. He recently shot an ad, while support from Sachin Tendulkar, Sunil Gavaskar and Kapil Dev continues.

title: US Senate Republicans reject effort to halt Trump's Iran war
content: US Senate Democrats are increasingly divided over arms sales to Israel, with over three dozen backing Bernie Sanders' resolution to halt them. Citing Prime Minister Netanyahu's "reckless decisions" and the expanded conflict, some Democrats shifted their stance. Despite bipartisan opposition, the measures were ultimately rejected, highlighting a growing internal party debate.

title: Before Osama, There Was Imad: How CIA-Mossad Hunted The Most Elusive Militant
content: Before Osama bin Laden became the global face of transnational terrorism, there was another figure-more elusive, less theatrical, but arguably more influential in the evolution of modern guerrilla warfare. He was Imad Mughniyeh.

content: The waivers on the purchase of Russian oil had allowed India to secure additional supplies amid global oil disruption. Reports suggest that Indian refiners have placed orders for roughly 30 million barrels of Russian oil during the period.

title: 12L households in 11 states/UTs self-enumerate for Census 2027
content: around 12 lakh households have self-enumerated so far for the houselisting operations (HLO) phase of census 2027 . more than half of these households availed of the facility in the past four-five days . the field operations will last a month - till May 15 .

title: The legacy of Jackie Robinson, baseball's greatest agent of change
content: baseball was his weakest sport . he was a much better basketball, football and track athlete than a baseball player . some say he's an even better tennis player than the greece Dodgers . "were there better baseball players in the Negro Leagues than Jackie?" he said .

title: Courts bound to accept community's beliefs, not judge them, TDB tells SC
content: the judicial test of the validity of a custom had to be confined to 'public order, health and morality' an individual's fundamental right must give way to centuries-old beliefs . the court is bound to accept the belief of the community, a senior advocate says .

title: National security collective duty of citizens, not just of forces: NSA Ajit Doval
content: national security is no longer the exclusive domain of uniformed personnel but the collective responsibility of every citizen . "together, our collective strength builds national morale," he said at the fifth convocation of Rashtriya Raksha University in Gandhinagar .

title: Nirav Modi lodges application with European human rights court to prevent removal to India
content: the 55-year-old jeweller has applied to the european court of human rights . he has lodged a rule 39 application to stay his removal to the ECtHR . his original appeal against extradition got thrown out on march 25 by the high court .

title: Attacks on merchant shipping completely unacceptable: EAM
content:
NEW DELHI: External affairs minister S Jaishankar Wednesday underlined India's strong commitment to safe and unimpeded transit passage of maritime shipping, saying attacks on merchant shipping were completely unacceptable. The minister gave vent to India's concerns related to merchant shipping disruptions in Strait of Hormuz, while participating in a meeting convened by Japan to discuss supply chain disruptions in energy markets. "Global growth demands that energy markets are not constricted. As a major energy consumer, India will work with like-minded partners to develop supply chain resilience," said Jaishankar in a post on X. He was participating in the Asia Zero Emission Community Plus Online Summit on Energy Resilience that was convened by Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi.

End of Article
